evidenceContracts.claim: The Rust retrieval evaluator owns the immutable corpus, deterministic metrics, candidate isolation, degradation report, and golden output.
The Rust Provider evaluator deterministically replays both supported protocols and matches the committed machine-readable golden report.
The credential-bearing live Provider smoke is excluded from the shipped deterministic verification contract.

retirementReview.outcome: A live credential-bearing Provider request is not a supported release-gating outcome.
retirementReview.reason: Offline fingerprinted Provider fixtures are authoritative and the milestone forbids credential reads and live Provider calls.

evidenceContracts.claim: Rust enforces provider-round, tool-call, elapsed-time, and tool-result byte ceilings before an extra effect is emitted.
Rust persists ordered tool request, decision, start, and terminal items in the session truth source before finalization.
Rust reconstructs, lists, resumes, continues, retries, compacts, and replays terminal sessions from one durable journal across process restarts.
The Rust CLI exposes explicit chat and Agent routes as separate typed commands without a feature-flag-driven implicit route switch.
Rust pauses for complete ordered tool calls, continues only after matching durable results, and terminates on provider-round exhaustion.
Rust recovery marks a started tool effect indeterminate and never re-executes it, while pre-start cancellation remains non-executing.
Both Provider protocols normalize every valid fixture to the same safe events without exposing private reasoning or provider detail.
The Rust renderer consumes shared runtime events, emits stable public terminal text, and strips terminal control sequences.
The Rust runtime rejects concurrent starts, persists exactly one truthful terminal for cancellation or failure, and rejects post-terminal events.
Exact aliases and commands win before deterministic BM25, normalization preserves mixed-language intent, and no-match queries stay empty.
Typed project, Skill, and MCP catalogs reject unsafe or drifted cards, preserve kind isolation, and report install or authorization readiness truthfully.
The Rust CLI exposes read-only capability, project, workspace, and Wiki status/search routes and rejects install, authorize, execute, or start flags.
BM25 runs first, verified embedding receives only lexical candidates, outsiders are rejected, and every semantic failure preserves BM25 order.
The immutable capability corpus rejects schema, identity, duplicate stable ID, expected-ID, fingerprint, and case-count drift.
Snapshot refresh rejects expected-hash, domain, and document-hash drift while preserving the last published file identity.
The Rust command parser recognizes every locked public command alias and argument shape while rejecting unknown or unsafe variants.
Continue creates a normal new terminal turn while retry creates a distinct terminal turn linked to an immutable source, and both survive replay.
Reviewed package orchestration verifies Rust authority ordering and the milestone release flow without implementing product behavior.
A terminal Rust session finalizes before restart, resumes deterministically, and reaches exactly one pinned Wiki workflow and current retrieval result.
Rust credential resolution exposes typed keyring states, never serializes secret values, and keeps headless operation keyring-free.
Optional embedding resources require exact identity, hash, ABI, CPU, architecture, model, vector, and catalog fingerprint proof before use.
Missing or unknown embedding-resource metadata fails closed without creating resources, while lexical retrieval remains independently available.
All supported Rust state routes write only normalized descendants of the single project-bound .minimax runtime root.
The three JavaScript diagnostic executors are TypeScript harness fixtures and are not shipped executable product behavior.

retirementReview.outcome: Legacy diagnostic child-process timing and output fixtures are absent from the shipped Rust command contract.
retirementReview.reason: They only support the transitional TypeScript diagnostic executor and remain hash-pinned solely until Phase 14 deletion.

evidenceContracts.claim: Responses and Chat Completions converge on the same safe ordered stream events with one truthful terminal and bounded public errors.
The TypeScript kernel fixture-construction helper is transitional harness plumbing rather than a shipped public or safety outcome.

retirementReview.outcome: The TypeScript-only kernel fixture-construction helper has no installed command or product-state outcome.
retirementReview.reason: Rust tests own kernel verification; this helper remains transitional source evidence until its Phase 14 removal.

evidenceContracts.claim: The discovered TypeScript test dependency graph is validated before imports and an independent Rust gate fails closed on evaluator reachability or unsafe local resolution.
The reviewed npm launcher performs only fixed platform-binary selection and never implements or falls back to product behavior.
Rust migration inventory and dry-run are deterministic, write nothing, and preserve the TypeScript-era source before any explicit apply.
Rust model selection uses strict secret-free configuration with CLI, environment, project, user, and default precedence and rejects unsafe values.
The selected provider, model, and protocol are embedded in the session and every turn request and survive exact journal replay.
The TypeScript npm diagnostic child-process executor is harness-only and is absent from the supported installed Rust command.

retirementReview.outcome: Spawning JavaScript diagnostic fixtures with timeout and truncation controls is not an installed Rust product outcome.
retirementReview.reason: The behavior exists only to test the retired TypeScript harness and has no documented public command or safety contract.

evidenceContracts.claim: Confirm mode preserves Provider order and call IDs and durably records request, decision, start, and terminal state before continuation.
Process-scoped full access skips only the prompt, still preflights and persists, and resets to confirm after restart.
Reviewed JavaScript archive orchestration packages only the fixed launcher and selected Rust binary without owning product behavior.
The compatibility harness executes Rust command, permission, Provider, retrieval, and product baselines against immutable fixtures.
Rust journal replay reconstructs session and turn state exactly, ignores identical duplicate records, and rejects conflicting identities.
Local Rust compaction is byte-stable and structured and excludes secrets, raw reasoning, failed partial output, frames, and tool bodies.
The TypeScript character-class token estimator is not used by the Rust runtime or exposed as a supported public contract.

retirementReview.outcome: Character-class token estimation and its local safety-margin heuristic are absent from the shipped Rust public contract.
retirementReview.reason: Rust uses explicit validated output limits and reported Provider usage rather than preserving an undocumented TypeScript heuristic.

evidenceContracts.claim: Rust persists every visible delta before publication, preserves partial evidence on cancellation, and finalizes the terminal outcome once.
Ink component dispatcher ownership and React unmount timing are not part of the shipped Rust terminal application architecture.

retirementReview.outcome: React and Ink mount, unmount, and injected-dispatcher ownership semantics do not exist in the Rust TUI.
retirementReview.reason: Rust owns terminal lifecycle through native state machines and guards; recreating React component timing would be filename parity only.

evidenceContracts.claim: Rust text and schema-v1 JSONL modes expose the same terminal outcomes and public exit classes for completed, interrupted, and failed runs.

responsibilities.rationale: The committed Rust retrieval evaluation proves the historical report is reproducible and matches the locked golden metrics.
